extraction.one_line_summary: VERONA Carlos won from 18-rider breakaway after ROGLIC attacked final climb, caught everyone except Verona, taking yellow by 44 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Movistar placed two riders (Verona, Mulberger) in 18-rider break then disrupted cooperation, drawing ELLISONDE's anger.
ROGLIC attacked final climb without team support after VINGEGAARD did prolonged pull, suggesting internal team hesitation on leadership.
VERONA survived as last breakaway rider with 13-second margin despite jumbo-Visma, INEOS, and UAE collective chase work.
Bahrain's tactic of LEON SANCHEZ dropping back from break to pace HAGEN/CARUSO completely failed on final climb.

rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: positive: Held off ROGLIC's surge solo on final climb after surviving breakaway politics all day
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Attacked final climb without team, nearly caught Verona solo, took yellow by 44s over VINGEGAARD
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: Did extended pull for ROGLIC then admitted afterwards he might have gone for stage himself, revealing team tension
rider_signals.ELLISONDE Kenny: negative: Wasted energy haranguing Movistar duo in breakaway instead of adapting to their disruption tactics
rider_signals.MULBERGER Gregor: positive: Movistar's breakaway disruptor executed team orders perfectly despite antagonizing other break members
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: neutral: Led chase attempt for AG2R but couldn't contain ROGLIC's acceleration on final climb
